headline: Transcript: Is the US economy really K-shaped?
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Claire Jones and Robert Armstrong
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Swamp Notes podcast episode: ‘Is the US economy really K-shaped?’

Marc FilippinoEveryone is talking about the economy.

Donald Trump voice clipI mean, we’re bringing prices down, but they came up with a new word, affordability.Marc FilippinoAnd there’s been one way a lot of people have been describing the economy lately.

[CLIPS PLAYING]

K-shaped economy.The K-shaped economy idea . . . The letter K . . .

Marc FilippinoWe’re gonna get into that K-shaped economy and unpack what it means for Washington.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is Swamp Notes, the weekly podcast from the Financial Times where we talk about all the things happening in US politics, finance and the economy. I’m Marc Filippino, and I hope you had a great Thanksgiving if you’re joining us from the States. This week, we’re asking: who is doing well in US President Donald Trump’s economy? Here with me to discuss is Rob Armstrong. He’s the FT’s US financial commentator. Hi, Rob.

Robert Armstro
